mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in real retail activity signals near-term household demand strength, raising expected corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not deliver higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than lower RSAFS growth in walk-forward tests, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment beyond its recent range signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its 6-month moving average by more than 0.3 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE breakouts are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if triggered returns are not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is rising while the federal funds rate is also high, discount-rate pressure dominates nominal revenue growth and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds its prior 12-month growth and FEDFUNDS is above 3.5%
evidence_refs: fact:2026-05 CPIAUCSL was 333.979 and FEDFUNDS was 3.63
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with sticky inflation
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-inflation high-FEDFUNDS trigger has nonnegative mean 20-day excess returns versus baseline, or if the interaction coefficient is not negative in walk-forward logistic tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth signals improving operating leverage and inventory demand, raising cyclical earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO growth crossovers are followed by below-baseline 20-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward evaluation, or if the positive-hit rate is less than the unconditional 65.5% rate.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp housing-starts decline signals weakening rate-sensitive demand and future construction spillovers, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average by more than 10%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside breaks do not have lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-break periods, or if triggered negative-signal precision is below 40% over two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Disinflation combined with lower policy rates reduces discount rates without imposing an immediate demand shock, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth falls below its 12-month growth and FEDFUNDS declines over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint disinflation and falling-FEDFUNDS condition fails to beat unconditional 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if its walk-forward positive-return frequency is below 65.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth with stable unemployment signals expanding income without a layoff cycle, supporting consumption and near-term earnings revisions and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE is unchanged or lower over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:2026-05 PAYEMS was 159001 and UNRATE was 4.3
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-stable interaction does not produce higher 5-day returns than PAYEMS growth alone, or if the interaction term is nonpositive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth combined with rising industrial production identifies broad expansion, improving aggregate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly growth is positive and INDPRO 3-month growth is positive
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive GDPC1 and INDPRO growth has lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than unconditional returns, or if out-of-sample Rank IC is below 0.03 in rolling 3-year windows.
